mnemonics: JPD-MC: Joint Probability Distributions Make Chains (to remember that joint probability distributions are foundational for understanding Markov Chains)
Memoryless Markov: My Memory Only Matters For My Next Step (to remember the Markov property that the future state depends only on the current state, not the entire history)
keyPoints: A joint probability distribution describes the probability of multiple random variables occurring together
A Markov chain is a stochastic process where the probability of moving to the next state depends only on the current state (memoryless property)
Transition probabilities define the likelihood of moving from one state to another in a Markov chain
commonMistakes: Assuming Markov chains have memory of past states (they don't - only current state matters)
Confusing joint probability P(A,B) with conditional probability P(A|B)
Forgetting to ensure that transition probabilities from any state must sum to 1
